# Mass file writes per process (ransomware impact)

**Dataset**: `xdr_data`

Ransomware encrypts files in bulk: count `ENUM.FILE` operations per (process, host) and keep the heavy hitters (`file_ops > 100` in a short window). A single non-backup process touching hundreds/thousands of files fast is the T1486 signature. Tune the threshold to the window; pair with a file-extension hunt (look for a single new extension appearing across many writes) to confirm encryption vs. normal bulk I/O. (Live-verified: this hunt surfaced real high-volume writers on the tenant.)

```sql
dataset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.FILE
| comp count() as file_ops by actor_process_image_name, agent_hostname
| filter file_ops > 100
| sort desc file_ops
```

_Query syntax live-verified on a real XSIAM tenant (narrow 30-min window). Adapt thresholds + the `contains`/`in` sets to your environment; filter early to keep the scan cheap._
